description: Showcasing specific product ingredients with their benefits and durability claims, emphasizing natural or premium components to build product credibility and drive purchase intent.
structural_recipe: 1) Open with product name in Arabic 2) List 2-3 key ingredients with their functional benefits (e.g., 'يدوم حتى ١٢ ساعة' for longevity) 3) Highlight natural/premium elements (rose water, lemon extract) 4) Include clear CTA directing to stores/e-commerce link in bio 5) Use Arabic-first copy with professional tone 6) Format as single carousel or static post with ingredient callouts
why_it_works: Saudi consumers value ingredient transparency and natural components as markers of quality and authenticity. Specific benefit claims (12-hour wear, rose water inclusion) appeal to cultural preferences for premium skincare with proven efficacy and traditional ingredients. The direct shopping link reduces friction in the purchase journey.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure ingredient claims comply with Saudi SFDA (Saudi Food and Drug Authority) regulations
Avoid overstating medical/therapeutic claims that may require regulatory approval
Verify all ingredients mentioned are actually in the product formulation
Consider local preference for traditional ingredients over synthetic-sounding components
cultural_constraints: Arabic language required for authenticity and trust
Direct purchase links and retail location mentions expected
